title: maesa calcarea
intro: Maesa calcarea, also known as the yellow ginger, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and edible roots. The roots of the Maesa calcarea plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Maesa calcarea include relief from digestive issues, fever reduction, and immune system support. Maesa calcarea is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and provide various health benefits.
